title: COMPAT — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations

sections.title: Market-specific context (relevant to constraint assessment)
sections.content:
| Factor | Value | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| Salt-air coastal environment | IEC 60721-3 severity C/3C3 | Standard IP54 BESS container needs IP55+ / stainless fasteners (incremental, not blocking) |
| Port peak loads | Cruise up to 16 MW, container ~7.5 MW | BESS size well within product scale (4.5 MW reference, modular to 25 MWh) |
| Regulatory driver | AFIR Reg. 2023/1804 — OPS by 2030 for TEN-T ports | Strong demand pull; public funding available |
| Critical-infrastructure status | NIS2, EU CER Directive | Enhanced supply-chain scrutiny on BESS components |
| Terminal land | Dense near quay, available in outer yards | Siting workable via modular placement |
| Fire regime | VdS + local Feuerwehr + ISPS security zones | Standard clearance design adapts |
| Grid connection | Typically 10–110 kV DSO/TSO, sometimes constrained near quay | BESS can itself relieve grid bottleneck |
| TAM | DE port-energy €500M–1B (2026–2032) | Economic case strongly supports CAPEX thresholds |

---

sections.title: Constraint × Market verdict matrix (9 assessments)
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Type | Verdict | Cost | Time | Rationale (short) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | operational | **mitigable** | medium | 6–12 mo | Port MV grids often saturated near quayside under OPS demand (cruise 16 MW). DSO upgrade process standard; BESS itself buffers shortfall. |
| 2 | Land Footprint and Siting | physical | **mitigable** | low | 3–6 mo | Container terminals dense but port authorities control ample outer-yard land; 40–80 m²/MWh fits within peripheral areas. Baugenehmigung via port master plan. |
| 3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | regulatory | **mitigable** | medium | 3–6 mo | ISPS security zones + dangerous-goods (bunkering) adjacency require specific separation. Standard VdS clearances plus port-fire coordination; well-established path. |
| 4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | physical | **mitigable** | low | 0–3 mo | Contained via standard NOVEC 1230 + deflagration venting already in base product. Port fire brigades dedicated; proximity to flammables requires mass-explosive-zone distance check only. |
| 5 | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | regulatory | **mitigable** | low | 6–12 mo | FGW TR8 type B plant cert required regardless of market — included in EPC scope. No port-specific delta. |
| 6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | economic | **mitigable** | medium | 3–6 mo | Ports = critical infrastructure under NIS2/CER. Public-procurement + port operators may require EU-origin cells or enhanced supplier due diligence. Premium cells available (ACC, Northvolt residuals, Verkor pipeline). |
| 7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | environmental | **none** | — | — | German coastal ports operate −10 to +35 °C year-round; well within −20 to +50 °C envelope. Salt-air (IP55+/stainless) is an enclosure upgrade, not an ambient-range breach. |
| 8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | physical | **none** | — | — | 10–15% AC-AC loss is structural to technology, not market-specific. Priced into OPEX for all use cases. |
| 9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | economic | **none** | — | — | AFIR mandate + EU funding + port-authority creditworthiness support €250–400/kWh envelope. Ports are mandate-driven buyers, not payback-gated C&I. |

---

sections.title: Knockouts
sections.content:
**None.** No constraint crosses the "physics impossibility" or "no certification path" bar for port operations.

False-knockout checks considered and rejected:
- *Salt-air corrosion* — not a knockout; IP55+ / 316 stainless fasteners are standard marine-grade upgrade (low incremental cost, <3 mo design change).
- *ISPS security clearance* — not a knockout; BESS placement within secured port perimeter is normal; integration into port access control is additive.
- *Adjacency to bunkering / dangerous goods* — mass-explosive safety distance per VdS is achievable through placement, not a physics wall.

---

sections.title: Incremental market-specific constraints (not in base list)
sections.content:
These are port-specific adaptations that extend the base constraints but do not rise to knockout:

| Adaptation | Mapped base constraint | Effort |
|---|---|---|
| Salt-air ingress protection (IP55+ / 316 stainless) | Ambient Temperature Envelope | LOW — <€50k / 2–3 mo design change |
| Grid-forming PCS for microgrid / islanding with OPS | Round-Trip Energy Losses (ops mode) | LOW — available from same PCS vendor (SMA, Sungrow, Power Electronics) |
| Harmonised port-EMS API (TOS / port-authority SCADA) | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | MEDIUM — €100–300k software interface work per port |

---

sections.title: Downstream impact
sections.content:
- Market **enters** Feature-Market Fit (Step 09), Competitive Landscape (Step 10), and Market Prioritization (Step 12).
- Mitigable-constraint cost penalty (sum of 6 × low-to-medium): ~€0.5–1.2M per deal, plus 6–12 mo lead time (already parallel to base EPC schedule).
- Key adaptation investment to load into prioritization: **IP55+ marine variant** (~€50k/6 mo one-off) + **port-EMS interface** (~€100–300k per first port, reusable).

---

rows.Rationale (short): Port MV grids often saturated near quayside under OPS demand (cruise 16 MW). DSO upgrade process standard; BESS itself buffers shortfall.
Container terminals dense but port authorities control ample outer-yard land; 40–80 m²/MWh fits within peripheral areas. Baugenehmigung via port master plan.
ISPS security zones + dangerous-goods (bunkering) adjacency require specific separation. Standard VdS clearances plus port-fire coordination; well-established path.
Contained via standard NOVEC 1230 + deflagration venting already in base product. Port fire brigades dedicated; proximity to flammables requires mass-explosive-zone distance check only.
FGW TR8 type B plant cert required regardless of market — included in EPC scope. No port-specific delta.

Ports = critical infrastructure under NIS2/CER. Public-procurement + port operators may require EU-origin cells or enhanced supplier due diligence. Premium cells available (ACC, Northvolt residuals, Verkor pipeline).
German coastal ports operate −10 to +35 °C year-round; well within −20 to +50 °C envelope. Salt-air (IP55+/stainless) is an enclosure upgrade, not an ambient-range breach.
10–15% AC-AC loss is structural to technology, not market-specific. Priced into OPEX for all use cases.
AFIR mandate + EU funding + port-authority creditworthiness support €250–400/kWh envelope. Ports are mandate-driven buyers, not payback-gated C&I.

rows.Effort: LOW — available from same PCS vendor (SMA, Sungrow, Power Electronics)
